1.11a: Address: TSB Bank PLC, Mortgages, PO Box 453, Mitcheldean, GL14 9LR
1.11b: Contact Mortgage Processing at the details provided in 1.11a
1.7: The lender will not lend where the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: The firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: The firm may act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower, subject to section 1.13.
3.1.3: Seller's solicitor details are only required upon specific request.
3.1.4: Transactions where the seller does not have legal representation are not accepted.
3.1.5: Acceptable List A documents: Current UK driving licence, Benefit payment book/Agency letter, Employer identity card with photo/signature (known employer).

Acceptable List B documents: Employer identity card with photo/signature (unknown employer), Old paper UK driving licence, HMRC Tax Code Notification, Home Office Residence Permit, Local council rent card or tenancy agreement.
3.2.3: Seller's solicitor details are only required upon specific request.
3.2.4: Transactions where the seller does not have legal representation are not accepted.
4.5b: Submit Certificate of Title via ECoT as per section 10.2b.
4.1: The valuation report is not supplied to the solicitor.
4.3: Refer to valuation report conditions in section 4.1.
5.1.1: Minimum ownership period for sellers is 6 months.
Remortgage applicants must have owned the property for at least 6 months, unless inherited (requires written confirmation).
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and immediate lease grants after freehold acquisition are not acceptable.
Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are not acceptable.
5.2.1: Assignable contracts and irrevocable powers of attorney for intervening sellers are not acceptable.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if conducted by qualified agents with indemnity insurance.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted subject to solicitor approval.
5.5.3a: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a regarding planning breach or unsatisfied conditions.
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission is not required.
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents is not required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required.
5.5.4: Report marketability or value restrictions to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
5.7.1a: Flying or creeping freeholds are accepted if they comprise only part of the title.
5.7.1c: Report flying freeholds to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
5.8.1: Buildings converted into max 4 flats where borrower is an occupier are acceptable.
If the borrower owns the freehold, a charge over the freehold title is required.
5.8.5: Mutual freehold arrangements for two leasehold flats are acceptable.
A mortgage is required over the borrower’s leasehold interest and the borrower's freehold interest in the other flat.
5.10.1: Report restrictions on use to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
5.13.1: Report second charges or non-funded purchase price balances to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
5.14.15a: Refer absentee or insolvent landlord cases to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is mandatory if the lender agrees to proceed with an absent or insolvent landlord.
5.14.17a: No documentation is required before completion, except as specified in 5.14.17b.
5.14.17b: Solicitors must report non-qualifying leaseholders and known/anticipated remediation costs under the Building Safety Act 2022.
Incorrect remediation or properties needing remediation must be reported immediately.
Completion must not occur without written instructions following such reports.
5.14.17c: Building Safety Act 2022 instructions apply to remortgages.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: 70 years at mortgage commencement and 30 years at mortgage redemption.
5.14.9: Pre-2005 builds: RPI/index-linked ground rents are acceptable; doubling ground rent is acceptable if the review period is 20 years or more; unacceptable if 19 years or less.
Post-Jan 2005 new build houses: Ground rent capped at 0.1% of Market Value per annum.
Post-Jan 2005 new build flats/maisonettes: Ground rent capped at the higher of 0.1% of Market Value or £250 per annum.
Second hand/resale properties: Ground rent capped at the higher of 0.1% of Market Value or £250 per annum.
5.14.10: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a for service charge matters.
5.14.11: Refer unsatisfactory lease terms to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts are not required.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service is not required.
5.15.2a: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a regarding management company issues.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates are not required.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms are not required.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association are not required.
5.16.2: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a if unable to certify search entries.
5.17.5a: Send a certified copy of the Power of Attorney after completion if used to execute the Mortgage Deed or Loan Agreement.
5.17.5b: Statutory declarations of non-revocation of power of attorney are not required.
5.19.1: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership.
5.20.1: Report solar PV panel roof space leases not meeting UK Finance minimum requirements to 1.11a.
5.20.3: Lender must be notified of solar PV roof space leases and the tenant's name.
A copy of the solar lease is not required.
5.20.4: Disclosure of Green Deal Plans is not required.
6.1.3: Report cases where the borrower is not taking up the offer via the SecureLink portal.
6.2.1: Report property description discrepancies to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
6.4.4: Report cash/financial incentives if they are 5% or more higher than stated in the Mortgage Offer.
Report all cash/financial incentives if none are listed in the Mortgage Offer.
Report all non-financial/in-kind incentives excluding fixtures and fittings.
Finders fees are not acceptable and must be reported.
Report if the deposit is paid by the seller on non-new build properties.
6.4.5: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a if control over purchase money is absent.
6.5.1: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a if vacant possession is not provided.
6.6.1: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a regarding let properties or Buy-to-Let eligibility.
6.6.2: Contact Mortgage Processing at 1.11a if current or completion-day letting details are unknown.
6.6.4: Refer Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
6.7.1: Acceptable schemes: NHBC, Premier Guarantee, Building Life Plans, LABC, Castle 10 (Checkmate), Buildzone, Global Home Warranties, International Construction Warranties, Protek, The Q Policy, Advantage HCI, Build Assure, One Guarantee, Aedis/HomeProof, ABC+, ARK, TMSC, CADIS.
6.7.2: New home warranty documentation is not required.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes are not required.
6.7.4: A Professional Consultant’s Certificate is required if no acceptable warranty scheme is in place.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates are not required to be sent.
6.8.1: Retention up to £1,000 for unadopted roads/sewers is pre-authorized.
Amounts exceeding £1,000 must be reported to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
6.9.1: Report absent easements to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
6.13.1: Report undischarged improvement or repair grants to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
7.3: Consent to mortgage is required from all occupants aged 17 or over.
7.4: Report doubts regarding disclosed information to Mortgage Processing at 1.11a.
8.1: Solicitors may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Only the indemnity insurance schedule is required after completion.
9.2: Indemnity insurance must cover the full value of the property.
10.2a: Remortgage advances are released in batches at 09:00, 11:00, 14:00, and 16:00.
10.2b: Minimum notice for funds: 5 working days.
Funds release occurs on the ECoT completion date; for release the day prior, that date must be specified as the completion date.
Interest accrues from the date funds are released.
10.3: Product fees added to the loan are not deducted from the advance amount released.
10.7: Delayed funds (M+9 digit refs) must be returned within 1 working day via CHAPS to Sort Code: 30-28-86, Account: 90000099.
Payment reference must be /RFB/ followed by the Mortgage Reference number.
2 working days' notice required for new completion date.
10.9: Notify Mortgage Processing at 1.11a immediately by telephone if completion is delayed.
10.10: Advance may be held for a maximum of 1 working day.
10.11: Interest is charged at the contractual rate from the date funds were sent if the return is delayed.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deeds are not required; solicitors must retain copies.
Electronic registration must include the Land Registry MD reference.
14.2.1: Required uploads via LMS: OS1/OS2/K17 (post-release), AP1 (within 30 days of completion), Mandatory Official Copy Register (post-registration).
Registration delays must be reported every 30 days.
14.2.2: Post-completion originals required: Deed of Guarantee, Title Indemnity, Certified Power of Attorney, Occupiers Waiver, Shared Ownership Undertaking/Deed of Assignment/Consent Clause, Title Information Document.
Title Information Document must be uploaded to STARS.
16.1.1: Title documents contact: 0345 835 3379 (requires 5 working days' notice).
Lender does not hold title documents for mortgages completed after February 2001.
16.3.2: Debt amount contact: Customer Service Centre (address in mortgage offer).
16.3.4: Transfer of equity only required if lender execution is necessary.
16.4.2: Proposed tenancy agreements are not required.
16.5.2: Deeds of variation, rectification, easement, or option agreements should be sent to the Mitcheldean address in 1.11a.
17.1.1: Redemption statements: tsb.co.uk/request-a-redemption-statement (2 working day turnaround).
17.2.1a: Repayment via CHAPS to Sort Code: 30-28-86, Account: 00000000.
Must quote mortgage account number and borrower name.
17.2.1b: Lender issues discharge via Land Registry EDS1.
